title: 10 best herbal creams for inflamed gums

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that help combat gum infections, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to the gums.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antioxidant properties: Tea tree oil's antioxidant properties help protect against gum tissue damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy gum development and reducing the risk of chronic conditions like gingivitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compound, terpinen-4-ol, effectively reduces bacteria and inflammation in the gums, promoting healthy tissue repair and regeneration. As a natural antiseptic, tea tree cream creates an ideal environment for the gums to heal, reducing redness, swelling, and pain, while also combating the underlying infection that caused the inflammation. This makes tea tree cream a popular and effective natural remedy for inflamed gums.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce gum inflammation and combat the underlying bacterial infection.

Terpinen-4-ol: As a major component of tea tree oil, terpinen-4-ol exhibits potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate gum inflammation and prevent further infection.

Cymene: Cymene, a phenolic compound in tea tree oil, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to the reduction of gum inflammation and the elimination of bacteria that can exacerbate the condition.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Contain oils with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to soothe inflamed gums.
Buds: Rich in melaleuca oil, which has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in healing and reducing inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh leaves and flowers of melaleuca alternifolia on a sunny morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried leaves and flowers in 4 cups of hot distilled water for 10 minutes.
Steep the infused liquid in a double boiler at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Combine 2 ounces of a base oil like coconut or olive oil with 1 teaspoon of the extracted essential oil mixture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like isoquinoline alkaloids, which help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in the cream, such as kaempferol and quercetin, combat oxidative stress and inflammation caused by gum disease, promoting healing and preventing further damage.

Wound-healing promotion: Kansas coneflower's triterpenoid saponins have been shown to stimulate wound healing processes, accelerating the recovery of damaged gum tissue and reducing the risk of infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have been shown to reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause infections. The cream's soothing and protective effects can help to calm inflamed gums, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia's ability to stimulate the immune system can aid in the body's natural healing process, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the gums, thereby alleviating inflamed gums.
Alkamides: Alkamides in Echinacea angustifolia creams have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm inflamed gums, promoting a healthier gum environment.

Polyphenols: The polyphenolic content in Echinacea angustifolia creams helps protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, contributing to gum health and inflammation reduction.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and combat infections in the gums.
Barks: The barks are used due to their astringent and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and protect inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root and clean it thoroughly with a fine mesh sieve.
Infuse 10 grams of the dried root in 100 milliliters of distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt them at 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the cooled infused liquid with 50 milliliters of aloe vera gel and 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil in the melted wax mixture.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and camphor, help to combat bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and infection, promoting healthy oral flora.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, which reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for periodontal disease.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to inflammation, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of gum disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum disease. Additionally, sage's antiseptic properties help to combat infections and promote a healthy environment for gum tissue to heal. This makes sage creams a natural and effective remedy for soothing inflamed gums and promoting oral health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflamed gums.
Salvianolic acids: A group of hydrophilic phenolic compounds that exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and protect inflamed gums.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the underlying causes of inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thujone and ursolic acid, that help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Stems: Contain astringent properties that help reduce swelling and ease inflammation in the gums.
Roots: Known for their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried salvia officinalis leaves using 500ml of carrier oil like coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours or until the oil has taken on a light green color and aroma.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth to separate the oil from the plant material in a bowl.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with the infused oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes or until it has thickened to a smooth cream consistency and cooled.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.
Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of ginger creams help combat the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi that can contribute to gum inflammation and infection.
Vasoactive compounds: Ginger creams contain vasoactive compounds like salicylates, which help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation in the gums, reducing swelling and promoting healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have potent bioactive effects that reduce inflammation and pain. The cream's topical application allows for direct delivery of these compounds to the affected area, providing localized relief from swelling, redness, and discomfort. This makes it an effective remedy for soothing inflamed gums, promoting healthy gum tissue, and maintaining overall oral health. Regular use can lead to improved gum health and reduced risk of gum disease.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly [6]-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Shogaols: Shogaols, a type of ginger-derived phenolic compound, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help combat gum infection and reduce inflammation.
Zingerone: Zingerone, a ginger-derived phenolic compound, has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate pain and swelling in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ginger are used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial actions that can help alleviate gum inflammation.
Buds: Ginger buds are used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them a potential ingredient in creams for inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 2 teaspoons of the dried root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the powdered zingiber officinale with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture for a 5-minute emulsification.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Antimicrobial: Turmeric's antibacterial and antifungal properties help combat gum disease-causing bacteria and fungi, promoting a healthy balance of oral microflora.
Wound healing: Turmeric's natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds aid in accelerating wound healing, reducing bleeding, and improving tissue repair in inflamed gums.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the breakdown of gum tissue. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the gum tissue allows it to target the root cause of inflammation, reducing swelling and pain. As a result, curcuma longa creams can help alleviate symptoms of gum inflammation and promote overall oral health. Regular use may lead to a healthier, more resilient gum line.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with inflamed gums.

Gingerols: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help to reduce pain and swelling in inflamed gums, and also have antimicrobial properties that prevent the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate gum inflammation.

Volatile oils (turmerones and atlantones): These terpenic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and pain in inflamed gums, and also have antimicrobial properties that prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain a high amount of curcuminoids, which are effective in reducing inflammation and pain.
Seeds: Some creams may use the seeds, which are rich in essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of finely grated curcuma longa root in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the curcuma longa infusion through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids.
Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the strained curcuma longa infusion with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and the melted beeswax in a bowl.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and emulsifies then pour it into a glass jar.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, which reduce swelling, redness, and pain in the gum tissue, providing relief from inflammation.
Antimicrobial agents: Aloe vera has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel contains enzymes, vitamins, and minerals that accelerate wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis, improving tissue hydration, and reducing scar formation, which can aid in the recovery of inflamed gums.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gum tissue. Additionally, Aloe vera has antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to gum inflammation. As a result, Aloe vera creams can provide quick relief and promote healing of inflamed gums.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin, a type of anthraquinone, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Catechin: Catechin, a type of phenolic compound, possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat bacterial infections and inflammation in the gums.
Glucomannan: Glucomannan, a type of polysaccharide, has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce inflammation and promote healing of damaged gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of aloe vera gel, which contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is widely used for its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in inflamed gums.

Aloe Vera Extract from Leaves: Aloe vera extract from the leaves is also frequently used in creams due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and protect inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of pure aloe vera gel from the inside of the aloe plant and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Mix 1/4 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Combine the aloe vera gel coconut oil and water mixture in a blender.
Add 2 drops of vitamin e oil and blend until well combined for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.

Antimicrobial properties: The essential oils present in lemongrass cream, such as limonene and alpha-pinene, exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi, making it effective against gum infections and inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing inflammation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause gum inflammation. Its astringent properties also help reduce bleeding and promote healing. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with inflamed gums, making it a popular natural remedy for gum health and maintenance. Regular use can lead to healthier gums and a reduced risk of gum disease.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators that contribute to gum inflammation.

Limonene: Exhibits antimicrobial properties that help reduce the bacterial load in the mouth, which can exacerbate gum inflammation, while also possessing anti-inflammatory properties to soothe inflamed tissues.
Beta-Caryophyllene: Demonstrates potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate gum inflammation, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of citral and geranial, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and heal inflamed gums.
Rhyzomes: Utilized for their citral and geranial content, similar to leaves, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Barks: Employed in lemongrass products for their essential oils, including citral and geranial, which aid in reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from the plant's upper parts for best essential oil content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for 2 hours to extract the essential oil in a still apparatus.
Combine 50g of beeswax, 50g of shea butter and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 10ml of the extracted cymbopogon citratus essential oil to the melted beeswax mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into glass containers and let it cool and set for 30 minutes to an hour before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial: The cream's antimicrobial properties combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to gum inflammation, reducing the risk of further complications and promoting healthy oral flora.
Wound-healing: Calendula's wound-healing properties enhance the natural process of tissue repair, helping to accelerate the healing of inflamed gums, reduce scarring, and promote a smoother recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpene saponins and flavonoids, reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth, thereby promoting healing and soothing irritated tissues. Additionally, calendula's ability to enhance blood flow and reduce swelling helps to alleviate discomfort and promote a healthy environment for gum tissue repair, making it an effective natural remedy for inflamed gums.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in inflamed gums.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Saponins: Saponins have antimicrobial properties that help control bacterial growth and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that help soothe and protect inflamed gums.
Leaves: They contain similar compounds to the flowers, providing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to aid in gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 cups of dried calendula officinalis flowers in 6 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes then let steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to separate the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the infused calendula liquid with the cooled beeswax mixture in a heat-proof bowl then stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids and polyphenols, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, promoting healing and reducing pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root extract has anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with inflamed gums.

Sothing and protective effects: Herbal licorice creams also contain soothing agents like aloe vera and chamomile, which help calm irritated gums and create a protective barrier against irritants, allowing the gums to heal more quickly.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, reduces swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators. It also has antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. Furthermore, the cream's astringent properties help tighten the gum tissue, reducing bleeding and promoting healing. This results in reduced inflammation, pain, and discomfort associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Liquiritin: A flavonoid glycoside, liquiritin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the gums from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation caused by bacterial infections.

Liquorice phenolics (specifically, isoliquiritin and glabrol): These phenolic compounds have been shown to have potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and protect inflamed gums.
Barks: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote healing in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Extract 5-10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 100ml of hot water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then combine the liquid with 100ml of glycerin.
Heat the mixture to 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes then let it cool to 40-50°c.
Mix 2-3 grams of beeswax with 20-30ml of distilled water in a separate container then heat it.
Combine the cooled mixture with the beeswax mixture and whip until it thickens and becomes a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects help to numb the pain and reduce swelling in inflamed gums, providing instant relief.
Eugenol: Eugenol's antibacterial properties help to combat the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth that can contribute to gum inflammation, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
Tannins: Tannins' astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, while also helping to soothe and calm inflamed gums by reducing their swelling and sensitivity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cooling sensation of peppermint can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Peppermint's active compounds, such as menthol and menthone, have antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections. Additionally, peppermint's refreshing properties can help reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a popular natural remedy for soothing inflamed gums and promoting oral health.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps to numb the pain and reduce inflammation in the gums by creating a cooling sensation and blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): It has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum inflammation.
Rosmarinic acid: It is a phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, prevent oxidative stress, and promote the healing of inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain menthol, a key compound that provides anti-inflammatory and soothing effects for inflamed gums.
Stems: Like leaves, stems contain menthol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and provide relief for irritated gums.
Roots: Roots of Mentha x piperita are rich in menthol and other bioactive compounds that help to alleviate inflammation and discomfort in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves when the plant is in full bloom for maximum potency.
Steep the leaves in 2 cups of distilled water for 10 to 15 minutes to release essential oils.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter in a double boiler to melt and combine.
Whip 1 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of glycerin and the herbal liquid to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame creams helps with inflamed gums because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as sesamin and sesamolin, have been shown to reduce swelling and soothe irritation in the gum tissue. Additionally, sesame creams often contain antimicrobial agents that help combat bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, it provides a gentle, non-invasive way to calm inflamed gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal sesame creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like sesamol, which reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antibacterial and antifungal agents: The cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy balance of oral bacteria and reducing the risk of infection.

Wound healing and tissue regeneration: Sesame oil, a key ingredient in herbal sesame creams, has been shown to enhance wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and accelerating cell proliferation, which helps to repair inflamed gum tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesamin, a powerful antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Rhyzomes: Contains sesamolin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Crush dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered sesamum indicum with 100 milliliters of distilled water to form a paste.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the paste thickens.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature, then add 5 grams of beeswax and stir until melted and well combined.
Pour the cream into sterile glass containers and store in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with inflamed gums because they contain a unique combination of natural ingredients that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The cream's main active compound, Podophyllotoxin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help calm irritated gum tissue, while other herbs like Calendula and Chamomile soothe and comfort the affected area, reducing swelling and discomfort. As a result, maidenhair tree creams provide fast and effective relief from inflamed gums, allowing for healthy oral function and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Saponin: Saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that may be contributing to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by gum inflammation, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair Tree contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair Tree are rich in tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the gums from further damage.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 20 grams of grated beeswax and 40 grams of coconut oil.
Infuse 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 100ml of sweet almond oil for 4 hours.
Add 5 grams of aloe vera gel and mix well to combine with the infused oil mixture.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized glass containers and let it set at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin and bisabolol present in chamomile have been shown to reduce swelling, pain, and redness associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, the cream's astringent nature helps to reduce bleeding and ease irritation, promoting a healthy oral environment. Regular application can also help to prevent future inflammation and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for maintaining good oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain caused by inflammation.
Antioxidants: Chamomile's antioxidants combat free radicals that can damage gum tissue, promoting healthy gum cell growth and reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate inflamed gums.

Soothing flavonoids: Chamomile's flavonoids have a calming effect on the mucous membranes, helping to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing sore and inflamed gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Flowers: Contain azulene compounds that possess potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making them effective against gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in amounts of about 20 grams for each 100ml cream recipe.
Combine the harvested flowers with enough witch hazel and glycerin to fill a clean glass jar leaving some headspace.
Store the mixture in a warm dark place for at least two weeks allowing for steeping and infusing of the herbs.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids after about 10 minutes of pressing.
Add a preservative such as vitamin e oil and beeswax to thicken the cream while also protecting it from spoilage taking about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with inflamed gums because they contain berberine, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets the source of gum inflammation. Berberine has been shown to reduce swelling and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and chemicals. Additionally, goldenseal's antibacterial properties help combat the underlying infections that contribute to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy balance of oral bacteria and reducing the severity of symptoms. Regular application of goldenseal cream can provide relief from inflamed gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation.
Beta-Sitosterol: Has anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of gum tissue damaged by periodontal disease or other oral infections.
Berberidin: Exhibits antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to prevent the growth of harmful microorganisms that can contribute to gum inflammation and infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, an alkaloid that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating gum inflammation.
Rhizomes: Contain berberastin, a compound that inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, helping to combat infections causing inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh goldenseal root hydrastis canadensis and clean it thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned goldenseal root with 500ml of organic coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 4 hours to extract the bioactive compounds from the goldenseal.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Add 10% beeswax by weight to the infused oil and stir until fully incorporated then pour into tubes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper creams helps with inflamed gums because of their unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbs used in these creams, such as tea tree oil and chamomile, work together to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, while also targeting the underlying bacterial causes of gum inflammation. Additionally, bell peppers are rich in antioxidants and vitamins that promote healthy gum tissue and prevent further damage. This natural approach can provide relief for people suffering from inflamed gums without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Certain herbs like tea tree oil, calendula, and eucalyptus have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to gum inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidant-rich herbs like green tea, rosemary, and sage may help neutralize free radicals that contribute to gum inflammation by reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of the bell pepper plant help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Fruits: The sweet and slightly bitter extracts from the fruit of the bell pepper plant have analgesic properties, reducing pain and discomfort associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum leaves and stems, ensuring adequate quantity for future use.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or low-temperature dehydrator for 24-48 hours.
Combine 20g of dried capsicum annuum with 100ml of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a warm water bath at 60°c for 2-3 hours, monitoring for optimal extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard solids, transferring resulting cream to sterile containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with inflamed gums because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The cream's active compounds, such as galactomannan, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing inflammation in the gum tissue. Additionally, fenugreek's antibacterial and antifungal activities help combat periodontal infections that can contribute to inflamed gums, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing symptoms associated with gingivitis and periodontitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fenugreek cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like galactomannans, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of fenugreek cream help protect gum tissues from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy gum function.

Wound healing promotion: Fenugreek cream's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve tissue elasticity helps accelerate wound healing in inflamed gums, reducing the appearance of bleeding, swelling, and discoloration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fenugreek roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds are employed because they contain mucilages that form a protective barrier on the gum tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry trigonella foenum-graecum seeds in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Grind 30 grams of dried trigonella foenum-graecum seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered trigonella foenum-graecum with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler, stirring constantly for 1 hour.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before adding 10 drops of tea tree essential oil and 5 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Transfer the cream mixture to a sterilized glass jar and store it in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle creams helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain. The creams contain anthocyanins and flavonoids, powerful antioxidants that inhibit the production of prostaglandins, responsible for causing inflammation. Additionally, roselle's antimicrobial compounds help combat bacterial infections, a common underlying cause of gum inflammation. By targeting both the inflammatory response and bacteria, roselle creams provide effective relief from inflamed gums, promoting healthy teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Roselle cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in roselle cream neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Antibacterial activity: Roselle cream has been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties against certain oral bacteria, including those responsible for periodontal disease, which can contribute to inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated gums, promoting healing and reducing pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers, beeswax, coconut oil, olive oil, and vitamin e oil in sufficient quantities for preparation.
Combine 20 grams of dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling water to create a strong decoction.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain and discard solids to produce a flavorful infusion.
Melt 50 grams of beeswax and blend it with 100 milliliters of coconut oil, 20 milliliters of olive oil, and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Gradually add the infused hibiscus sabdariffa mixture to the melted oil mixture while stirring constantly, allowing it to cool and thicken for about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with inflamed gums because they contain a potent blend of herbs that target the underlying causes of gum inflammation. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of ingredients like willow bark and ginger help to reduce swelling and pain, while the antibacterial compounds in plants like neem and myrrh combat the harmful bacteria that can contribute to gum disease. By addressing these root issues, bitter orange creams provide a soothing and effective solution for inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal bitter orange creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids and limonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the gum tissue and prevent further damage to the gums.

Antibacterial properties: Bitter orange extracts have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain bacteria that can contribute to gum disease, helping to reduce the severity of inflamed gums.

Gum-tissue strengthening compounds: Herbal bitter orange creams often contain flavonoids like hesperidin and naringenin, which help strengthen the connective tissue in the gums and promote healing, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Barks: Contain tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm irritated gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried citrus peel from organic bitter orange trees in bulk quantities.
Combine one part citrus peel with two parts coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at 160°f for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids, adding 10% lavender essential oil by volume.
Transfer the resulting cream to sterilized glass jars, sealing and labeling with contents and preparation dates.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with inflamed gums because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The thymol present in thyme has been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause gum disease, including gingivitis and periodontitis. When applied topically as a cream, it helps soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and swelling, while also promoting healthy tissue growth and strengthening the gums. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from inflamed gums without harsh chemicals or antibiotics.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Thyme's essential oils, such as thymol, have antibacterial activity against a wide range of bacteria, including those responsible for gum disease, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.

Antioxidant property: Thyme's antioxidant compounds, including vitamin E and beta-carotene, can help protect the gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the thyme plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help soothe irritated gums and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality thyme leaves from a trusted source weighing approximately 10 grams.
Dry the fresh thyme leaves in a warm oven at 60°c for four hours or until crumbly.
Combine 20 grams of dried thyme powder with 40 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and mix well.
Allow the mixture to steep for eight hours in a cool dark place before straining through cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix equal parts of the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil, then heat at 65°c for three minutes while stirring until fully melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with inflamed gums because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Lavender oil contains flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can ease irritation and discomfort, promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment. This natural approach can provide relief from inflamed gums without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Lavender's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to inflamed gums by preventing the spread of infection and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender cream can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthier gum environment.

Antioxidant property: Lavender's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and exacerbate inflammation, promoting overall oral health and well-being by protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe gum pain.
Buds: Contains essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and leaves for infusion in hot water.
Steep the herb mixture for 10-15 minutes to release active compounds and oils.
Mix the infused liquid with melted coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency using an electric mixer.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for inflamed gums are topical treatments containing natural ingredients that help reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and promote healing. They work by soothing the inflamed area, reducing inflammation, and preventing further irritation. The best herbal creams for inflamed gums include Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, Salvia officinalis, Cinchona officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, inflamed gums can lead to painful chewing, difficulty speaking, and social withdrawal, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed gums that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations such as eczema, acne, and rosacea. These creams often contain soothing herbs like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile, which calm inflammation, reduce redness, and promote healing. Additionally, some herbal creams may be used to treat minor burns, wounds, and cuts by promoting tissue repair and reducing the risk of infection.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with inflamed gums is a blend of Aloe vera, Tea tree oil, and Echinacea. Aloe vera soothes and calms inflammation, while Tea tree oil's antibacterial properties help combat infections. Echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory, reduces swelling and promotes healing. Apply these creams topically to the affected area, ideally after brushing and flossing. Regular use can help alleviate symptoms and promote gum health. Consult a dentist before making any changes to your oral care routine.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Iranian Journal of Basic Medical Sciences, herbal creams can be beneficial for inflamed gums due to the effectiveness of certain herbal products in reducing gingival inflammation and bleeding. The study, which reviewed clinical trials on herbal treatments for gingivitis, found that herbs such as pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak, as well as polyherbal formulations like triphala, significantly reduced plaque index, bleeding index, microbial count, and biomarkers of inflammation. By incorporating these herbal ingredients into creams, individuals with inflamed gums may experience relief from symptoms and improved oral hygiene, making herbal creams a promising option for managing gingivitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with inflamed gums because of its powerful antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea tree oil, derived from the leaves of this plant, has been shown to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, it helps to soothe and calm the gums, promoting a healthy environment for healing and reducing discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate gum inflammation and promote oral health.

Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The main compound, eucalyptol, has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and ease pain. This makes it a popular ingredient in oral care products. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into gum tissue allows for effective reduction of inflammation, promoting healthy gum tissue and a decrease in symptoms such as redness and sensitivity. Regular application can lead to improved gum health and overall oral well-being.

Echinacea purpurea creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects help stimulate the body's natural defenses, fighting off the underlying infection causing the inflammation. This natural approach can provide relief from redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with inflamed gums, promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment. Regular application can also help soothe and calm irritated tissues.

The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the gums. These properties promote healing, soothe irritation, and prevent further irritation, allowing the gums to recover quickly. Additionally, aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, further aiding in their recovery and overall health.

Additionally, calendula's soothing effects can calm irritated tissues, promoting healing and reducing pain. Salvia officinalis creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as carnosic acid and ursolic acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause infections. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has natural antioxidant properties that help to protect gum tissue from damage. Cinchona officinalis creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain. The cream's quinine content has natural antibacterial properties that combat infection and promote healing. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis contains tannins, which have astringent properties that help tighten the gum tissue and reduce bleeding. The combination of these properties makes Cinchona officinalis creams an effective treatment for inflamed gums, promoting a faster recovery and healthier gum tissue. Ginkgo biloba creams helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties and antioxidant capabilities. The extract from the ginkgo biloba leaf has been shown to improve blood flow to the gums, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tissue repair. Additionally, ginkgo biloba's ability to inhibit oxidative stress and free radical damage helps to soothe and calm irritated gum tissue, providing relief from swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with inflamed gums. Zingiber officinale creams helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound, gingerol, present in the cream, reduces swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, its antibacterial properties combat the underlying causes of gum inflammation, such as bacterial infections. By soothing and calming the gums, Zingiber officinale creams promote a healthy and balanced oral environment, reducing the risk of further complications and promoting overall gum health. Curcuma longa creams helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound, curcumin, is extracted from the turmeric root and has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums. By blocking the production of inflammatory enzymes, curcumin helps to alleviate swelling, redness, and pain associated with inflamed gums. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to promote oral health and reduce gum inflammation, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution.
